headline: Intel’s CPU Comeback Lifts All Boats — Can AMD and ARM Ride the Same AI Wave?
summary: When Intel (NASDAQ:INTC) released its Q1 2026 earnings last week, the semiconductor sector got a clear signal: the AI demand story holds firm, and it’s expanding beyond GPUs into CPUs. Intel’s overall revenue reached $13.6 billion, up 7% year-over-year from $12.7 billion, beating expectations handily. Its Data Center and AI (DCAI) segment delivered $5.1 billion, ... Intel’s CPU Comeback Lifts All Boats — Can AMD and ARM Ride the Same AI Wave?

headline: After 11 years, Northland Capital downgrades AMD
summary: Investing.com -- Northland Capital Markets has downgraded Advanced Micro Devices to Market Perform after more than a decade of bullish coverage, setting a price target of $260 and warning that consensus estimates for 2027 are too optimistic.

content.title: After 11 years, Northland Capital downgrades AMD
content.summary: Investing.com -- Northland Capital Markets has downgraded Advanced Micro Devices to Market Perform after more than a decade of bullish coverage, setting a price target of $260 and warning that consensus estimates for 2027 are too optimistic.

headline: Lightmatter Names Roy Kim Vice President of Product to Lead Global Deployment of Photonic Interconnects
summary: MOUNTAIN VIEW, Calif., April 27, 2026--Lightmatter, the leader in photonic (super)computing, today announced the appointment of Roy Kim as Vice President of Product. Kim brings more than 15 years of AI infrastructure product leadership from Google, AMD, and NVIDIA, and will lead Lightmatter's product strategy as the company scales its Passage™ photonic interconnect platform and Guide™ VLSP light engine from landmark industry firsts to volume deployment across the world's most advanced AI data ce

headline: This Week In AI Chips - WeRide Advances Multi-Chip Integration With Major Partnerships
summary: WeRide Inc. has announced the multi-chip compatibility of its WeRide Driving (WRD 3.0) ADAS solution, achieving integration across leading computing platforms like NVIDIA, Qualcomm, and SiEngine. This development enables original equipment manufacturers (OEMs) to deploy an extended range of L2++ ADAS solutions, from high-performance to cost-efficient models, signaling a significant shift toward the democratization of these advanced driving technologies. At Auto China 2026, WeRide solidified...

headline: Google, Meta, Microsoft, Amazon, Apple earnings: What to expect
summary: Alphabet (GOOG, GOOGL), Meta (META), Microsoft (MSFT), Amazon (AMZN), and Apple (AAPL) are all reporting quarterly earnings results this week. D.A. Davidson head of technology research Gil Luria joins Yahoo Finance to discuss his outlook for the results and the questions to which he'll be looking for answers.

content.title: Google, Meta, Microsoft, Amazon, Apple earnings: What to expect
content.description:
Alphabet (GOOG, GOOGL), Meta (META), Microsoft (MSFT), Amazon (AMZN), and Apple (AAPL) are all reporting quarterly earnings results this week.

D.A. Davidson head of technology research Gil Luria joins Yahoo Finance to discuss his outlook for the results and the questions to which he'll be looking for answers.
